6.0.reason: Not listed in the official FLAC3D 6.0 command indexes.

keywords.name: displacement-shear
keywords.description: Overwrites the shear displacement vector for all interface nodes in the range. The value will be constrained to be perpendicular to the interface normal direction.

keywords.name: displacement-small
keywords.description: Interfaces operating in small-strain mode derive their forces from a comparison of “virtual positions” of the two interacting faces, where a “virtual position” of a point is the original coordinate of the point plus the accumulated displacement (displacement-small) to date. If one contacting object is removed (e.g., backfill within a tunnel), and another substituted (e.g., a liner is installed) using the same interface, then large initial stresses may appear because the two sides of the interface appear to interpenetrate (because the virtual position of the old object has changed with respect to the specified position of the new object).

keywords.name: extra
keywords.description: Set the extra interface node variable value for array index i.

keywords.name: group
keywords.description: Add group name s to the interface nodes in the range. Use of the group logic is described in Groups. This command may take the form zone interface node "slotname = groupname" — quotation marks required, spaces at equals sign ignored — where slotname is the slot assignment and groupname is the group name.

keywords.name: group-remove
keywords.description: Remove s from interface nodes. If a slot is not specified, the group name is removed from all slots it is found in.

keywords.name: group-slot
keywords.description: Assign the group to the slot named s. The default slot name is Default.

keywords.name: history
keywords.description: Interface variables can be sampled for interface s at the interface node specified. See the zone history command. Note that this command does not take a following range. The following keywords are defined:

keywords.name: history-displacement-normal
keywords.description: normal displacement (positive values indicate increased penetration).

keywords.name: history-displacement-normal-id
keywords.description: Indicates the history should be taken at interface node ID i in interface s. If a specific interface was not indicated then an interface name must be specified following.

keywords.name: history-displacement-normal-position
keywords.description: Indicates the history should be taken at the node closest to position v among the nodes of interface s. If a specific interface was not indicated than the nearest node on any interface will be found.

keywords.name: history-displacement-shear
keywords.description: shear displacement magnitude

keywords.name: history-displacement-shear-id
keywords.description: Indicates the history should be taken at interface node ID i in interface s. If a specific interface was not indicated then an interface name must be specified following.

keywords.name: history-displacement-shear-position
keywords.description: Indicates the history should be taken at the node closest to position v among the nodes of interface s. If a specific interface was not indicated than the nearest node on any interface will be found.

keywords.name: history-stress-normal
keywords.description: normal stress (Compressive stresses are positive)

keywords.name: history-stress-normal-id
keywords.description: Indicates the history should be taken at interface node ID i in interface s. If a specific interface was not indicated then an interface name must be specified following.

keywords.name: history-stress-normal-position
keywords.description: Indicates the history should be taken at the node closest to position v among the nodes of interface s. If a specific interface was not indicated than the nearest node on any interface will be found.

keywords.name: history-stress-shear
keywords.description: shear stress magnitude

keywords.name: history-stress-shear-id
keywords.description: Indicates the history should be taken at interface node ID i in interface s. If a specific interface was not indicated then an interface name must be specified following.

keywords.name: history-stress-shear-position
keywords.description: Indicates the history should be taken at the node closest to position v among the nodes of interface s. If a specific interface was not indicated than the nearest node on any interface will be found.

keywords.name: initialize-stresses
keywords.description: Initializes the normal and shear forces on all interface nodes in the range and in contact with a surface face. The values of the forces are determined by the tractions on the host face of the node. This means that if zone stresses are initialized, then interface forces can be initialized to be approximately compatible, thus reducing the amount of cycling to reach initial equilibrium.

keywords.name: list
keywords.description: List information about interface nodes in the range.

keywords.name: list-displacement
keywords.description: The accumulated displacement, shear displacement, and small-strain displacement of the nodes.

keywords.name: list-extra
keywords.description: The values stored in extra index i of the interface nodes.

keywords.name: list-host
keywords.description: The host zone face, and the weighting values used for the attachment.

keywords.name: list-information
keywords.description: The position, normal direction, and effective area, and contact tolerance.

keywords.name: list-property
keywords.description: The value of interface properties at the nodes. The available property names are:

keywords.name: list-property-bonded-slip
keywords.description: flag indicating if slip is allowed.

keywords.name: list-property-cohesion
keywords.description: cohesion in units of stress

keywords.name: list-property-cohesion-residual
keywords.description: residual cohesion. If assigned a value greater than or equal to zero, this value is assigned to cohesion after frictional shear failure.

keywords.name: list-property-dilation
keywords.description: dilation in degrees

keywords.name: list-property-friction
keywords.description: friction in degrees

keywords.name: list-property-friction-residual
keywords.description: residual friction. If assigned a value greater than or equal to zero, this value is assigned to friction after frictional shear failure.

keywords.name: list-property-shear-bond-ratio
keywords.description: shear bond strength as a ratio of the tensile strength.

keywords.name: list-property-stiffness-normal
keywords.description: normal stiffness in units of stress over displacement.

keywords.name: list-property-stiffness-shear
keywords.description: shear stiffness in units of stress over displacement.

keywords.name: list-property-tension
keywords.description: tensile strength in units of stress

keywords.name: list-property-tension-residual
keywords.description: residual tensile strength. If assigned a value greater than or equal to zero, this value is assigned to tension after frictional shear failure.

keywords.name: list-state
keywords.description: current state of the interface node

keywords.name: list-stress
keywords.description: shear and normal stresses and shear stress direction

keywords.name: list-target
keywords.description: target face information including penetration

keywords.name: list-velocity
keywords.description: velocity at nodes

keywords.name: stress-shear
keywords.description: sets the incremental shear stress v for all interface nodes in the range.

keywords.name: stress-normal-increment
keywords.description: Sets the normal stress increment to f for all interface nodes in the range. Note that compressive stresses are positive. The normal stress increment is added to the normal stress calculated from interface penetration.

keywords.name: property
keywords.description: Assigns one or more new property values to all interface nodes in the range.

keywords.name: property-bonded-slip
keywords.description: Slip is allowed, or not allowed, for a bonded interface segment. Default is off (i.e., slip is not allowed if bond is intact). This is only applicable if a bonded interface is specified (tension is set).

keywords.name: property-cohesion
keywords.description: cohesion [stress]

keywords.name: property-cohesion-residual
keywords.description: residual cohesion [stress]. If a value greater than of equal to zero is specified, this value is assigned to cohesion after frictional shear failure. Default is -1.0.

keywords.name: property-dilation
keywords.description: dilation angle [degrees]

keywords.name: property-friction
keywords.description: friction angle [degrees]

keywords.name: property-friction-residual
keywords.description: residual friction angle [degrees]. If a value greater than of equal to zero is specified, this value is assigned to friction after frictional shear failure. Default is -1.0.

keywords.name: property-shear-bond-ratio
keywords.description: The shear bond strength is set to sbr times the normal bond strength (tension). Note that giving sbratio alone does not cause a bond to be established; the tensile bond strength must also be set. The default value for sbratio is 100 (i.e., shear bond is 100 times tensile bond). This is only applicable if a bonded interface is specified (tension is set).

keywords.name: property-stiffness-normal
keywords.description: normal stiffness [stress/displacement]

keywords.name: property-stiffness-shear
keywords.description: shear stiffness [stress/displacement]

keywords.name: property-tension
keywords.description: tensile strength [stress]

keywords.name: property-tension-residual
keywords.description: residual tensile strength [stress]. If a value greater than of equal to zero is specified, this value is assigned to tension after frictional shear failure. Default is -1.0.

keywords.name: update
keywords.description: This command prevents the search for new contacts after movement occurs on an interface. The same contacts are preserved, whatever the magnitude of displacement. Use with caution, because physically unrealistic behavior can result if displacements are large. The default behavior is on, which allows normal searching for new and broken contacts.

keywords.name: add
keywords.description: Add the specified value to the existing value.

keywords.name: gradient
keywords.description: Apply a gradient to the scalar-value provided.

keywords.name: multiply
keywords.description: Multiply the existing value by the specified value.

keywords.name: vary
keywords.description: Apply a linear variation to the scalar-value provided.

keywords.name: displacement-shear
keywords.description: Overwrites the shear displacement vector for all interface nodes in the range. The value will be constrained to be perpendicular to the interface normal direction.

keywords.name: displacement-small
keywords.description: Interfaces operating in small-strain mode derive their forces from a comparison of “virtual positions” of the two interacting faces, where a “virtual position” of a point is the original coordinate of the point plus the accumulated displacement ( displacement-small ) to date. If one contacting object is removed (e.g., backfill within a tunnel), and another substituted (e.g., a liner is installed) using the same interface, then large initial stresses may appear because the two sides of the interface appear to interpenetrate (because the virtual position of the old object has changed with respect to the specified position of the new object). The stored displacements associated with a particular interface s may be multiplied by a factor f , using the keyword displacement-small . Normally, this factor will be zero, thus restoring the virtual positions of the faces comprising the interface “host” to their original locations. In this case, a new interacting object will not cause initial stresses to be generated when its coordinates match the original coordinates of the interface host faces. However, it is possible to give f a nonzero value, in order to simulate an initial lack-of-fit between the two contacting objects. The command changes the virtual positions in the interface, and in the two faces that are in contact. However, for a newly created interface, the “target” face is not found until cycling has begun. Therefore, for all positions to be reset, at least one cycle should be executed before giving the command if the target (i.e., the face without the interface elements) has some existing deformation.

keywords.name: extra
keywords.description: Set the extra interface node variable value for array index i.

keywords.name: group-slot
keywords.description: Add group name s to the interface nodes in the range. Use of the group logic is described in Groups . This command may take the form zone interface node "slotname = groupname" — quotation marks required, spaces at equals sign ignored — where slotname is the slot assignment and groupname is the group name. An interface node may belong to many groups in different slots, up to a maximum of 128. The group range element can be used to select objects by group name. The following optional keywords are available: Remove s from interface nodes. If a slot is not specified, the group name is removed from all slots it is found in. Assign the group to the slot named s . The default slot name is Default .

keywords.name: history-stress-shear-position
keywords.description: Interface variables can be sampled for interface s at the interface node specified. See the zone history command. Note that this command does not take a following range. The following keywords are defined: normal displacement (positive values indicate increased penetration). Indicates the history should be taken at interface node ID i in interface s . If a specific interface was not indicated then an interface name must be specified following. Indicates the history should be taken at the node closest to position v among the nodes of interface s . If a specific interface was not indicated than the nearest node on any interface will be found. shear displacement magnitude Indicates the history should be taken at interface node ID i in interface s . If a specific interface was not indicated then an interface name must be specified following. Indicates the history should be taken at the node closest to position v among the nodes of interface s . If a specific interface was not indicated than the nearest node on any interface will be found. normal stress (Compressive stresses are positive) Indicates the history should be taken at interface node ID i in interface s . If a specific interface was not indicated then an interface name must be specified following. Indicates the history should be taken at the node closest to position v among the nodes of interface s . If a specific interface was not indicated than the nearest node on any interface will be found. shear stress magnitude Indicates the history should be taken at interface node ID i in interface s . If a specific interface was not indicated then an interface name must be specified following. Indicates the history should be taken at the node closest to position v among the nodes of interface s . If a specific interface was not indicated than the nearest node on any interface will be found.

keywords.name: initialize-stresses
keywords.description: Initializes the normal and shear forces on all interface nodes in the range and in contact with a surface face. The values of the forces are determined by the tractions on the host face of the node. This means that if zone stresses are initialized, then interface forces can be initialized to be approximately compatible, thus reducing the amount of cycling to reach initial equilibrium. Note that since normal stress is determined by absolute penetration, initialization of normal stresses is accomplished by setting the stress-normal-increment value for each node affected.

keywords.name: list-velocity
keywords.description: List information about interface nodes in the range. The accumulated displacement, shear displacement, and small-strain displacement of the nodes. The values stored in extra index i of the interface nodes. The host zone face, and the weighting values used for the attachment. The position, normal direction, and effective area (in 3D) or length (in 2D), and contact tolerance. The value of interface properties at the nodes. The available property names are: flag indicating if slip is allowed. cohesion in units of stress residual cohesion. If assigned a value greater than or equal to zero, this value is assigned to cohesion after frictional shear failure. dilation in degrees value of shear displacement (in units of length) at which the dilation becomes zero. friction in degrees residual friction. If assigned a value greater than or equal to zero, this value is assigned to friction after frictional shear failure. shear bond strength as a ratio of the tensile strength. normal stiffness in units of stress over displacement. shear stiffness in units of stress over displacement. tensile strength in units of stress residual tensile strength. If assigned a value greater than or equal to zero, this value is assigned to tension after frictional shear failure. current state of the interface node shear and normal stresses and shear stress direction target face information including penetration velocity at nodes

keywords.name: stress-shear
keywords.description: sets the incremental shear stress v for all interface nodes in the range.

keywords.name: stress-normal-increment
keywords.description: Sets the normal stress increment to f for all interface nodes in the range. Note that compressive stresses are positive. The normal stress increment is added to the normal stress calculated from interface penetration.

keywords.name: property-tension-residual
keywords.description: Assigns one or more new property values to all interface nodes in the range. Slip is allowed, or not allowed, for a bonded interface segment. Default is off (i.e., slip is not allowed if bond is intact). This is only applicable if a bonded interface is specified ( tension is set). cohesion [stress] residual cohesion [stress]. If a value greater than of equal to zero is specified, this value is assigned to cohesion after frictional shear failure. Default is -1.0. if shear displacement reaches this value [in length unit, default value is infinity], the dilation becomes zero. dilation angle [degrees] friction angle [degrees] residual friction angle [degrees]. If a value greater than of equal to zero is specified, this value is assigned to friction after frictional shear failure. Default is -1.0. The shear bond strength is set to sbr times the normal bond strength (tension). Note that giving sbratio alone does not cause a bond to be established; the tensile bond strength must also be set. The default value for sbratio is 100 (i.e., shear bond is 100 times tensile bond). This is only applicable if a bonded interface is specified ( tension is set). normal stiffness [stress/displacement] shear stiffness [stress/displacement] tensile strength [stress] residual tensile strength [stress]. If a value greater than of equal to zero is specified, this value is assigned to tension after frictional shear failure. Default is -1.0.

keywords.name: update
keywords.description: This command prevents the search for new contacts after movement occurs on an interface. The same contacts are preserved, whatever the magnitude of displacement. Use with caution, because physically unrealistic behavior can result if displacements are large. The default behavior is on , which allows normal searching for new and broken contacts.

keywords.name: add
keywords.description: Add the specified value to the existing value.

keywords.name: gradient
keywords.description: Apply a gradient to the scalar-value provided. Note the origin keyword is optional, however, the default value (0,0,0) is used if the origin keyword is not included in the command — which may not produce the intended result.

keywords.name: multiply
keywords.description: Multiply the existing value by the specified value.

keywords.name: vary
keywords.description: Apply a linear variation to the scalar-value provided.
